text.content:
This system implements Neon City Overdrive and the other Action Tales games by Peril Planet.

To pick your game, open Game Settings → Configure Settings → Neon City Overdrive and other Action Tales! and set the Game Line (Neon City Overdrive, Star Scoundrels, Dungeon Crawlers, Hard City, or Tomorrow City). Then click Apply Recommended Settings at the top of the same page to switch the optional rules to that game's recommended configuration.

Click the lock icon at the top right of a character sheet to toggle between edit mode (build the character) and play mode (click Tags to roll).

  • Trademarks are your character's defining talents. Each has a list of Triggers; a Trigger upgraded to an Edge becomes a clickable Tag. Trademarks can be built directly on the sheet or authored as Items and dragged on.
  • Flaws (two slots), Conditions, and Traumas are negative Tags — clicking one adds a Danger die. Adding a new Trauma automatically rolls a death check (optional rule).
  • Gear: everyday equipment goes in the freeform Basic Gear box; Special Gear items carry their own Tags and an optional damage track.
  • Tracks: Hits, Stunt Points, XP, and (optionally) Stash and Drive. Left-click a box to fill, right-click to clear — or switch to fill-to-level clicking in the system settings.
  • Click the Stunt label to spend a Stunt Point; the ways it can be used are posted to chat.

Checks are built in a shared roll pool that every player sees. Click Tags on any sheet to add dice:

  • A positive Tag (Trademark name, Edge, positive Gear/Scene/Threat Tag) adds an Action die.
  • A negative Tag (Flaw, Trauma, active Condition, negative Tag) adds a Danger die.
  • Shift-click any Tag to invert it. A Threat's Danger Rating circle adds that many Danger dice at once.
  • The dialog's Bonus / Penalty buttons add ad-hoc situational dice, and every check always includes one base Action die.

Click Roll: each Danger die cancels one matching Action die, and the highest surviving Action die is the result — 6 is a success (extra 6s are boons), 4–5 a partial success at a cost, 3 or less a failure, and a botch when nothing (or only 1s) survives.

For a quick roll outside the shared pool, use the dice button on the character sheet, or type /r a4d2 in chat (4 Action dice against 2 Danger dice).

The GM-side elements are lightweight and all feed the same shared roll pool:

  • Threats (Actors): a Danger Rating, a Hits track, Tags, and freeform Drive/Actions text. The Boss toggle triples a Threat's Hits.
  • Vehicles (Actors): a Danger Rating, Hits, Tags, and inline Conditions/Criticals.
  • Scenes (Items): a name and a list of Tags for the current situation or location.
  • Jobs (Items): the mission planning hub — Concept, Objective, Obstacles, and Link, plus linked Scenes and Threats dragged onto the sheet. Their Tags, Danger Ratings, and Hits tracks are all usable inline, so a GM can run a whole Job from one window.
  • Pressure (optional rule): a shared GM track pinned near the top of the screen; each uncancelled 6 on a Danger die ticks it up by one.
  • Phase-based initiative (optional rule): characters roll a d6 and act before the Threats on 4+ or after on 3 or less; the combat tracker shows the phases as color-coded sections with a turn-taken toggle per combatant.

Everything configurable lives under Game Settings → Configure Settings → Neon City Overdrive and other Action Tales!

The Game Line setting re-themes every sheet, dialog, and chat card and swaps in that game's terminology (e.g. Stunt Points become Flow in Star Scoundrels and Moxie in Hard City / Tomorrow City). Changing it requires a reload.

Apply Recommended Settings switches the optional rules to the recommended configuration for the selected line in one click: the Drive and Stash tracks for Neon City Overdrive, Pressure for Star Scoundrels, Ties for Hard City, Cred/Ties/Advantages for Tomorrow City, and so on. Each rule can still be toggled individually afterwards.

Other settings control starting Hits and Stunt Points, the starting Conditions new characters are seeded with, the Stunt Point options listed in chat, track lengths, and how clicking a track behaves.
